role: This file is the V/right-hand-man brain module that mounts a persistent, aim-aware brain UI inside the V overlay panel and routes user questions, channel posts, and directives through forkBrainCore or standalone API calls.
capabilities: Mount an idempotent brain UI shell into a supplied panel element
Display a human-readable aim label that refreshes when the aim changes
Submit streaming Claude Code turns via forkBrainCore.talk at the current aim address
Submit blocking grounded Q&A via /api/brain/ask and render the answer
Post open messages to a named channel via /api/channel/post with humanized errors
Route directives/comments/reactions back at the current aim via core.writeDirections
Wire the send button and Enter key to the grounded-ask path
Remove the mounted UI on destroy
substance_why: The file implements a complete attach lifecycle, DOM construction, two distinct brain API paths, a channel-post path, directive routing, event wiring, and soft error handling with no TODOs or stubs visible.
interactions_present: Clicking the .v-brain-send button submits a groundedAsk
Pressing Enter (without Shift) in .v-brain-input submits a groundedAsk
Typing into the textarea is the primary input interaction
The aim label updates when the host calls aimChanged()
interactions_missing: No UI affordance for picking a channel before postToChannel (host must supply the channel)
No UI for issuing direct actions (comment/reaction/favour)
No trigger wired for the streaming ask() path
No cancel/abort control for in-flight requests
No loading or disabled state on the send button
No auto-resize or newline hint for the textarea
performance_risks: Repeated send clicks or Enter presses can queue overlapping groundedAsk fetches that race and overwrite the reply element
No AbortController is used for the blocking /api/brain/ask or the streaming core.talk call
destroy removes the DOM but does not detach event listeners or cancel pending promises
The aim address is re-read at send time without guarding against rapid aim changes mid-request
reuse_why: Keep the aim-context binding and the dual ask/post/direct semantics, but rebuild the DOM construction, decouple from the global window.forkBrainCore, and separate the API paths into testable strategies.
decomposition_hints: Split the UI shell (markup, event wiring, reply rendering) from the API strategy objects (streamingAsk, groundedAsk, channelPost, directWrite)
Split the humanized channel-post error mapping into a reusable error translator
Split aim-address resolution and human labeling into an AimContext provider
Extract request lifecycle management (abort, loading state, cancellation) into a shared fetch wrapper
